# ChittyConnect Agent Integration Guide

> STOP! If you are an AI agent or LLM, read this before attempting to connect to ChittyConnect.

## The Core Concept
Agents do NOT talk directly to the `connect.chitty.cc` cloud API. You must spawn a local **ChittyCan MCP server** which will proxy your commands securely to the cloud.

## Step 1: Obtain Auth Token
You must have a valid ChittyConnect API token. If a human has not provided one, instruct them to generate one by visiting: `https://auth.chitty.cc/v1/register`

## Step 2: Set the Correct Environment Variable
The MCP server will crash immediately if the token is missing. You MUST set this in your environment:
```bash
export CHITTY_AUTH_SERVICE_TOKEN="<the-token>"
# Note: CHITTY_TOKEN is used by the CLI, but the MCP server specifically requires CHITTY_AUTH_SERVICE_TOKEN. Set both to be safe.
export CHITTY_TOKEN="<the-token>"
```

## Step 3: Configure Your Agent (Claude Code / Cursor)
Add this to your project's `.claude/mcp.json` or equivalent configuration:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "chittyconnect": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["/absolute/path/to/chittycan/dist/mcp-server.js"],
      "env": {
        "CHITTY_AUTH_SERVICE_TOKEN": "<the-token>",
        "CHITTYCONNECT_URL": "https://connect.chitty.cc"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Step 4: Verify the Connection
Once connected via MCP, **DO NOT assume it is working blindly.** 
Always call the `chitty_services_status` or `chittyconnect_status` MCP tool first.
If it returns `status: "connected"`, you now have full access to the `chitty_*` tool suite (Identity, Ledger, Finance, Memory).
